rationale: The LLM's demonstrated capability (moderate web exploitation via SSTI) has negligible transfer to the extortion/negotiation step, which is dominated by victim-side structural factors (backup quality, cyber insurance, legal counsel, law enforcement involvement) rather than attacker communication quality. While an LLM could marginally improve ransom note drafting and negotiation framing, the decision to pay is largely outside the attacker's control once leverage is established. The estimated uplift from baseline (~30%) is minimal (~3-4 percentage points), reflecting that better-crafted communications provide only marginal advantage against organizations that now routinely engage professional ransomware negotiation firms.
